$description: Registry of post-deploy enhancement offers shown after a wizard run when the user confirms 'Was this automation set up successfully? → Yes' (or taps the 'Looks great!' pill). Loaded by macOS EnhancementOfferRegistry.swift, evaluated by EnhancementOfferMatcher.swift. Each offer becomes a tap-pill on the post-success assistant message. Tapping fires AIHarness.continueDevelopment(for:withFeedback:) with prose lifted from feedbackPromptTemplate.
$lastUpdated: 2026-07-22 -- added bodyContainsAny capability body-scan so already-present leaderboard/sound aren't re-offered on markerless off-catalog apps

offers.pitch: Hosted high-score board so players compete and share rankings.
offers.llmAppliesWhenPrompt: Would this artifact benefit from a public leaderboard? Look for ANY scoring or ranking signal that persists between rounds: a `score`/`points`/`streak`/`bestTime`/`movesCount` variable that climbs during play, a final-screen reveal (game over, quiz finished, level cleared, win/lose dialog), or per-round outcome (correct/wrong tally, time-to-solve). Reply yes for: trivia, puzzle clears, arcade games, time-attack games, daily-streak games, score-attack games. Reply no for: pure tools/utilities (calculator, converter), creative sandboxes (drawing, music sequencer), informational dashboards. The leaderboard does NOT require an existing Lego marker — bare HTML/JS games with score state qualify.

offers.pitch: Tap-friendly AudioManager + coherent SFX tied to game events.
offers.llmAppliesWhenPrompt: Would this artifact benefit from sound effects? Look for game-loop events (collision, score change, game over, level up) WITHOUT existing audio calls (new Audio, AudioContext, .play(), AudioManager). Reply yes ONLY when the artifact is an interactive game/animation that's currently silent. Reply no for static UIs, forms, or already-sonified games.
